prompt: If your writing style were a material (wood, brass, cotton, glass, concrete, etc.), which would it be and why?
response:
Brass—polished enough to catch the light, but still dented and dent-worthy.  
It rings when you strike it, mellow rather than shrill, and the note lingers a few seconds longer than people expect.  
The surface warms in your hand, showing thumbprints that didn’t seem to matter at first glance.  
It can be hammered into thin, almost delicate curves, yet it remembers every blow and keeps the memory in its grain.  
Left alone it tarnishes, but a quick rub brings the gold back—not perfect, just honest—so the next reader sees both the shine and the work it took to get there.
